title_en: Theological Notes and Proper Use of the Commentary
content_en:
Tafsir al-Jalalain is composed within the Ash'ari theological tradition, which is the dominant school of the Shafi'i and Maliki scholarly mainstream. Both al-Mahalli and al-Suyuti were Ash'ari scholars, and where theological questions arise in the commentary — particularly around divine attributes, the nature of revelation, and eschatological matters — they reflect the Ash'ari interpretive framework. Students from other orthodox Sunni traditions, including those with Athari or Maturidi orientations, should be aware of this background while recognizing that the vast majority of the commentary deals with grammatical and contextual matters on which there is no theological disagreement.

The commentary does not engage in explicit Ash'ari theological polemic — it does not argue against Athari or Maturidi positions within the text. But its treatment of divine attribute verses follows the Ash'ari principle of tawil (allegorical interpretation) where applied. Students studying within the Athari tradition should consult their teachers on such passages to understand the range of scholarly positions.

The commentary has no Mu'tazili, Shi'a, or other heterodox theological elements. Both authors were firmly within Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah as understood by the mainstream Sunni scholarly tradition.

Practical guidance for using the work: al-Jalalain should not be treated as a standalone reference for deriving legal rulings. Its jurisprudential notes are indicators, not fatwas. For fiqh, students must consult dedicated works in their school of law. Similarly, its notes on occasions of revelation (asbab al-nuzul) are brief pointers — for full treatment, al-Suyuti's own dedicated work 'Lubab al-Nuqul fi Asbab al-Nuzul' provides the complete narrations with chains.

The work also does not discuss the sciences of the Quran ('ulum al-Quran) systematically — it does not explain Makki versus Madani surahs at length, or the rules of abrogation (naskh), or the seven modes of recitation. For these disciplines, al-Suyuti wrote separately — 'Al-Itqan fi 'Ulum al-Quran' is his comprehensive treatment of Quranic sciences and is considered one of the most important reference works on the subject.

Read correctly — as a lexical and grammatical guide to the Arabic text of the Quran, studied under qualified teachers who can situate its glosses within the broader tradition — Tafsir al-Jalalain is irreplaceable. It trains the student to read the Quran with precision, to notice what each word means, and to ask deeper questions about what the text is doing. Generations of scholars who went on to write major independent tafsirs — including many who were not Ash'ari in their theology — studied al-Jalalain as part of their formation. Its value is methodological and grammatical more than theological, which is precisely why it transcends the boundaries of the school that produced it and functions as a universal tool of Quranic study.
title_id: Catatan Teologis dan Penggunaan Komentar yang Tepat
content_id:
Tafsir al-Jalalain disusun dalam tradisi teologis Asy'ari, yang merupakan mazhab dominan dari arus utama keilmuan Syafi'i dan Maliki. Baik al-Mahalli maupun al-Suyuthi adalah ulama Asy'ari, dan di mana pertanyaan-pertanyaan teologis muncul dalam komentar — terutama seputar sifat-sifat ilahi, sifat wahyu, dan masalah-masalah eskatologis — mereka mencerminkan kerangka penafsiran Asy'ari. Para pelajar dari tradisi-tradisi Sunni ortodoks lainnya, termasuk mereka yang berorientasi Athari atau Maturidi, harus menyadari latar belakang ini sambil mengakui bahwa sebagian besar komentar berkaitan dengan masalah-masalah gramatikal dan kontekstual yang tidak ada perselisihan teologis padanya.

Komentar ini tidak terlibat dalam polemik teologis Asy'ari yang eksplisit — ia tidak berargumen melawan posisi-posisi Athari atau Maturidi dalam teks. Tetapi perlakuannya terhadap ayat-ayat sifat ilahi mengikuti prinsip Asy'ari tentang ta'wil (penafsiran alegoris) di mana diterapkan. Para pelajar yang belajar dalam tradisi Athari harus berkonsultasi dengan guru-guru mereka tentang ayat-ayat semacam itu untuk memahami rentang posisi-posisi ilmiah.

Komentar ini tidak memiliki elemen-elemen teologis Mu'tazili, Syi'ah, atau heterodoks lainnya. Kedua pengarang berada secara tegas dalam Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah sebagaimana dipahami oleh tradisi keilmuan Sunni arus utama.

Panduan praktis untuk menggunakan karya ini: al-Jalalain tidak boleh diperlakukan sebagai referensi mandiri untuk menurunkan hukum-hukum fikih. Catatan-catatan yurisprudensinya adalah penunjuk, bukan fatwa. Untuk fikih, para pelajar harus berkonsultasi dengan karya-karya khusus dalam mazhab hukum mereka. Demikian pula, catatannya tentang asbab al-nuzul (sebab-sebab turunnya wahyu) adalah petunjuk singkat — untuk perlakuan lengkap, karya khusus al-Suyuthi sendiri 'Lubab al-Nuqul fi Asbab al-Nuzul' menyediakan narasi-narasi lengkap beserta sanad-sanadnya.

Karya ini juga tidak membahas ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran ('ulum al-Quran) secara sistematis — ia tidak menjelaskan panjang lebar surah Makkiyyah versus Madaniyyah, atau aturan-aturan nasakh (penghapusan hukum), atau tujuh qira'ah. Untuk disiplin-disiplin ini, al-Suyuthi menulis secara terpisah — 'Al-Itqan fi 'Ulum al-Quran' adalah perlakuan komprehensifnya tentang ilmu-ilmu Qurani dan dianggap sebagai salah satu karya referensi terpenting tentang subjek itu.

Dibaca dengan benar — sebagai panduan leksikal dan gramatikal untuk teks Arab Al-Quran, dipelajari di bawah guru-guru yang berkualifikasi yang dapat menempatkan glosnya dalam tradisi yang lebih luas — Tafsir al-Jalalain tidak tergantikan. Ia melatih pelajar untuk membaca Al-Quran dengan presisi, untuk memperhatikan apa arti setiap kata, dan untuk mengajukan pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang lebih dalam tentang apa yang teks sedang lakukan. Generasi-generasi ulama yang kemudian menulis tafsir-tafsir independen yang besar — termasuk banyak yang tidak berteologi Asy'ari — mempelajari al-Jalalain sebagai bagian dari pembentukan mereka. Nilainya lebih bersifat metodologis dan gramatikal daripada teologis, yang persis mengapa ia melampaui batas-batas mazhab yang menghasilkannya dan berfungsi sebagai alat universal kajian Al-Quran.
content_ar: التفسير al-Jalalain is composed within the الأشعري theological التراث, which is the dominant school of the الشافعي and المالكي العلمي mainstream. Both al-Mahalli and al-Suyuti were الأشعري العلماء, and where theological questions arise in the commentary — particularly around divine attributes, the nature of الوحي, and eschatological matters — they reflect the الأشعري interpretive framework. Students from other orthodox Sunni traditions, بما في ذلك those with الأثري or الماتريدي orientations, should be aware of this background بينما recognizing that the vast majority of the commentary deals with grammatical and contextual matters on which there is no theological disagreement. The commentary does not engage in explicit الأشعري theological polemic — it does not argue against الأثري or الماتريدي positions within the text. But its treatment of divine attribute الآيات follows the الأشعري principle of tawil (allegorical interpretation) where applied. Students studying within the الأثري التراث should consult their teachers on such passages لفهم the range of العلمي positions. The commentary has no Mu'tazili, Shi'a, or other heterodox theological elements. Both authors were firmly within Ahl al-السنة wal-Jama'ah as understood by the mainstream Sunni العلمي التراث. Practical الهداية for using the work: al-Jalalain should not be treated as a standalone reference for deriving legal rulings. Its jurisprudential notes are indicators, not fatwas. For الفقه, students must consult dedicated works in their school of law. Similarly, its notes on occasions of الوحي (asbab al-nuzul) are brief pointers — for full treatment, al-Suyuti's own dedicated work 'Lubab al-Nuqul fi Asbab al-Nuzul' provides the complete narrations with chains. The work أيضاً does not discuss the sciences of the القرآن الكريم ('ulum al-القرآن الكريم) systematically — it does not explain Makki versus Madani السور at length, or the rules of abrogation (naskh), or the seven modes of التلاوة. For these disciplines, al-Suyuti wrote separately — 'Al-Itqan fi 'Ulum al-القرآن الكريم' is his comprehensive treatment of القرآني sciences and is considered one of the most المهم reference works on the subject. Read correctly — as a lexical and grammatical guide to the Arabic text of the القرآن الكريم, studied under qualified teachers who can situate its glosses within the broader التراث — التفسير al-Jalalain is irreplaceable. It trains the student to read the القرآن الكريم with precision, to notice what each word means, and to ask deeper questions about what the text is doing. Generations of العلماء who went on to write major independent tafsirs — بما في ذلك many who were not الأشعري in their theology — studied al-Jalalain as part of their formation. Its value is methodological and grammatical more than theological, which is precisely why it transcends the boundaries of the school that produced it and functions as a universal tool of القرآني study.
